title: Fix MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline to Ignore Embedded Fields

statement.markdown: Write a MongoDB aggregation pipeline to ignore embedded fields in a collection. The pipeline should only project the 'name' and 'age' fields from the 'user' collection, ignoring any nested fields like 'address' or 'phone'. For example, if a document looks like this: `{ 'name': 'John', 'age': 30, 'address': { 'street': '123 Main St', 'city': 'Anytown', 'state': 'CA', 'zip': '12345' } }`, the pipeline should output `'name': 'John', 'age': 30`.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a MongoDB collection with documents containing various fields, including 'name' and 'age'.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be an array of objects, each containing the 'name' and 'age' fields from the corresponding input document.
statement.notes: The pipeline should handle documents with missing or null 'name' or 'age' fields without throwing an error.

constraints.description: Maximum number of documents in the input collection

examples.explanation: The pipeline should ignore the 'address' field and only project the 'name' and 'age' fields.

hints.text: Use the '$project' stage to specify the fields to include in the output.
Use the '$and' operator to combine multiple conditions in the '$project' stage.

editorial.title: Fixing the MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline
editorial.intuition: The pipeline should use the '$project' stage to specify the fields to include in the output, and use the '$and' operator to combine multiple conditions.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force solution is to use multiple '$project' stages to exclude each field individually.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a single '$project' stage with the '$and' operator to exclude all nested fields.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution will correctly ignore all nested fields and only project the 'name' and 'age' fields.
editorial.dryRun: To test the solution, run the pipeline on a sample collection and verify that it produces the expected output.
commonMistakes: Including unnecessary fields in the '$project' stage
